name: Nelson Aldrich Rockefeller
description: Vice President of the United States from 1974 to 1977
abstract: Nelson Aldrich Rockefeller (July 8, 1908 – January 26, 1979) was the 41st vice president of the United States, serving from 1974 to 1977 under President Gerald Ford. A member of the Republican Party and the wealthy Rockefeller family, he was the 49th governor of New York from 1959 to 1973.

concerts.label: Program Commemorating the Second Anniversary of the Hungarian Revolution

about_quotes.speaker: Dan Morgenstern
about_quotes.evidence: The publisher was Dolly Schiff – Dorothy Schiff – who was a – she was somebody who took a hand in the paper. ... Up until the day before the election, suddenly she insisted that the Post come out for Nelson Rockefeller

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: And there was a woman named Evelyn Cunningham who was a real figure in New York civil rights history, who worked for Nelson Rockefeller.
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: Nelson Rockefeller was a great fan of mine... he walked on to shake my hand

about_quotes.speaker: Russell Procope
about_quotes.evidence: Nelson Rockefeller was in charge of Latin affairs at that time, and so we went around and we all did this together, Kirby and everybody, and we pulled all sorts of strings to get out of the country, down on this job.
